characters.name: Alannah Curtis
characters.description: Alannah CurtisInformationFull NameAlannah CurtisGenderFemaleOccupationSinger-SongwriterFamilyUnnamed fatherUnnamed motherRomancesUnnamed ex-boyfriendGunnar Scott(ex-boyfriend)Avery Barkley(ex-boyfriend)AffiliationsThe Last HighwaysInformationFirst EpisodeWhere the Night GoesLast EpisodeBeyond the SunsetPortrayed ByRainee BlakeAlannah Curtisis a singer-songwriter who is a newcomer on the Nashville music scene. Introduced in thefinal seasonepisodeWhere the Night Goes, she meetsWill Lexington,Avery BarkleyandGunnar Scottafter it is suggested byBucky Dawesthat they could use a female singer in their lineup. They invite Alannah to join the band and they prove to be a huge hit, although tension arises when the public start focusing on her as the main star of the group. Gunnar finds himself attracted to her and they end up sleeping together after she tells him about her parents, saying she got her love of country music from her mother. However, he soon becomes jealous of her maintaining contact with an ex-boyfriend and tensions within the band are soon at an all time low. It also turns out thatBrad Maitlandwishes to poach her away from the group, which she objects to.

characters.name: Albert
characters.description: AlbertInformationGenderMaleOccupationEmployee at Wyatt IndustriesInformationFirst EpisodePilotLast EpisodeBefore You Go Make Sure You KnowPortrayed ByJeremy ChildsAlbertis an employee of Wyatt Industries. Albert is involved in discussions withLamar Wyattand his daughterTandyas to usage of some land that Lamar had planned to be the site of a new stadium. When Lamar is hospitalized for a heart attack, Albert brings Tandy some information so that she can get up to date as CFO, and convinces her that taking the time to do so is what Lamar would want her to do.

characters.name: Allyson Del Lago
characters.description: Allyson Del LagoInformationFull NameAllyson Del LagoGenderFemaleOccupationNurseFriendsJuliette BarnesInformationFirst EpisodeThe Wayfaring StrangerLast EpisodeA Little Bit StrongerPortrayed ByJen RichardsAllyson Del Lagois a nurse who assistsJuliette Barnesin the aftermath of her plane crash. She is briefly seen in the hospital attending to Juliette beforeRayna Jaymesarrives for a visit.  She helps Juliette with her exercises and suggests using a walking aid since Juliette is so determined to get her mobility back.  However, Juliette ends up having flashes back to her plane crash and they bond over their ability to stick at what they are doing.  Allyson reveals that she is transgender and later tells Juliette that she informed her father, who did not take the news well.

characters.name: Alyssa Greene
characters.description: Alyssa GreeneInformationFull NameAlyssa GreeneGenderFemaleResides inSilicon Valley, CaliforniaOccupationMarketing expertEye ColorBrownHair ColorBrownInformationFirst EpisodeA Change Would Do You GoodLast EpisodeReasons to QuitPortrayed ByRachel Bilson Alyssa Greeneis a Silicon Valley marketing expert and the chief strategy officer forHighway 65. Introduced in theSeason FiveepisodeA Change Would Do You Good, she meets everyone at the Highway 65 offices and tells them that the way to move the label forward is by striking a deal to market products with well-known companies. These include having The Exes market baby cribs since Scarlett is pregnant.

characters.name: Amelia Rose George
characters.description: Amelia Rose GeorgeInformationFull NameAmelia Rose GeorgeGenderFemaleFamilyDamien George(father)Scarlett O'Connor(mother)Doug O'Connor(maternal grandfather)Beverly O'Connor†(maternal grandmother)Deacon Claybourne(maternal great-uncle)InformationFirst EpisodeYou Can't Lose Me(mentioned) Amelia Rose Georgewas to be the first-born child ofDamien GeorgeandScarlett O'Connor, conceived during their brief relationship. Damien was not fully prepared to be a father at first when a paternity test revealed that he was the biological parent and eventually Scarlett broke off their relationship. Gunnar decided to help Scarlett with being a parent to the child but when they were mugged at the supermarket Scarlett was pushed stomach first into a car and she ultimately suffered a miscarriage.  However, the doctor told her that this was quite normal and would not have necessarily been caused by the assault.

characters.name: Avery Barkley
characters.description: Avery BarkleyInformationFull NameAvery Silas BarkleyGenderMaleResides inNashvilleOccupationSinger/SongwriterMusic ProducerEye ColorBlueHair ColorBrownFamilyCarl Barkley(father)Sandra Barkley(mother)Unnamed SisterJuliette Barnes(wife)Cadence Barkley(daughter)Eddie Barnes†(father-in-law)Jolene Barnes†(mother-in-law)Unnamed childRomancesScarlett O'Connor(ex-girlfriend)Marilyn Rhodes(fling)Juliette Barnes(wife)Layla Grant(ex-girlfriend)FriendsGunnar Scott(bandmate)Scarlett O'ConnorWill Lexington(bandmate)Alannah Curtis(bandmate)EmilyRayna Jaymes†Deacon Claybourne(boss)Bucky Dawes(manager; currently)InformationFirst EpisodePilotLast EpisodeBeyond the SunsetPortrayed ByJonathan Jackson Avery Barkleyis a singer/songwriter and music producer. He is portrayed byJonathan Jackson. Avery first appears backstage withScarlett O'Connorwhen he bumps intoJuliette Barneson their way to seeRayna Jaymes. He apologizes and she tells him to do it slower next time. She then walks away while he gazes after her (prompting a punch from then-girlfriend Scarlett, who witnesses the gaze). This coincidental meeting turns out to be a pivotal event, being the first encounter Avery has with Juliette, whom he later befriends and provides critical support to.

characters.name: Beverly O'Connor
characters.description: Beverly O'ConnorInformationFull NameBeverly O'ConnorGenderFemaleDate of Death2015Resides inMississippiEye ColorBlueHair ColorBlondeFamilyGideon Claybourne(father)Deacon Claybourne(brother)Doug O'Connor(husband)Scarlett O'Connor(daughter)Maddie Conrad(niece)Rayna Jaymes†(sister-in-law)RomancesDoug O'Connor(husband)InformationFirst EpisodeCrazyLast EpisodePlease Help Me, I'm Fallin'Portrayed ByDana Wheeler-NicholsonBeverly O'Connor(néeClaybourne) was the mother ofScarlett O'Connorand the sister ofDeacon Claybourne. She felt betrayed by Deacon and Scarlett because they didn't tell her she had a niece,Maddie Conrad. In one episode, Scarlett dedicates the song "Black Roses" to her mother in sound check. She appeared angry while Scarlett sang it. After the song, Beverly asks why she did that and pushes Scarlett up against a brick wall, hurting her and causing her to become mentally unable to perform. When Deacon was diagnosed with cancer, she was initially reluctant to help her brother but eventually relented.  She lied about being told that she was an unsuitable match for her brother but when the truth came out they went through with the operation which ended up putting her in a coma.  Deacon blamed himself for her condition but Beverly eventually awoke.  Sadly, this wasn't to last as she suffered another aneurysm and Scarlett made the difficult decision to take her off life support against Deacon's wishes.

She felt betrayed by Deacon and Scarlett because they didn't tell her she had a niece,Maddie Conrad. In one episode, Scarlett dedicates the song "Black Roses" to her mother in sound check. She appeared angry while Scarlett sang it. After the song, Beverly asks why she did that and pushes Scarlett up against a brick wall, hurting her and causing her to become mentally unable to perform. When Deacon was diagnosed with cancer, she was initially reluctant to help her brother but eventually relented.  She lied about being told that she was an unsuitable match for her brother but when the truth came out they went through with the operation which ended up putting her in a coma.  Deacon blamed himself for her condition but Beverly eventually awoke.  Sadly, this wasn't to last as she suffered another aneurysm and Scarlett made the difficult decision to take her off life support against Deacon's wishes.

He is first seen in theSeason ThreeepisodeIs The Better Part Overwhen he shows up to see his son perform. However, he is unaware about his son's true sexuality and makes him uncomfortable while they eat together in a diner. He later expressed disgust after|Willcame out in a press conference.

characters.name: Brad Maitland
characters.description: Brad MaitlandInformationFull NameBrad MaitlandGenderMaleOccupationRecord label bossFamilyJake Maitland(son)RomancesJessie Caine(ex-wife)Unnamed wifeInformationFirst EpisodeThe Night Before (Life Goes On)Last EpisodeBeyond the SunsetPortrayed ByJeffrey Nordling Brad Maitlandis the successful, charming, egotistical and narcissistic owner of the most successful record label in town.  He is the ex-husband ofJessie Caineand father of their sonJake. His name is first mentioned in the episodeGhost in This Houseby Zach who tells Deacon thatJessie Cainewas previously married to him. She later reveals that he was abusive to both her and their son and had an affair with her former assistant who was also her best friend.

characters.name: Brad Maitland

characters.name: Brent McKinney
characters.description: Brent McKinneyInformationGenderMaleResides inNashvilleOccupationBusinessmanRomancesWill Lexington(fling)FriendsLayla Grant(client)InformationFirst EpisodeI'll Never Get Out of This World AliveLast EpisodeWe've Got Things to DoPortrayed ByDerek Krantz Brent McKinneyisLayla Grant's former publicist and had a brief relationship withWill Lexington. He is shown to be a highly diverse character taking on differing aspects as needed. In this way he is somewhat of a social chameleon. He is shown to be a highly diverse character taking on differing aspects as needed. In this way he is somewhat of a social chameleon. He is also capable of being a ruthless business man, using only logic and numbers to make decisions. On the other side of the story. he is also incredibly caring about anyone who he considers a friend, supporting them even while he disapproves. Brent is an intelligent character as he is able to anticipate action, and react to it quickly, which is the probable cause of his high rank within Edgehill. He is also proud, he is not afraid to flaunt his homosexuality in front of others and show off his partner, though will not fight those who disapprove as he is a pacifist. He is also in love withWill Lexington.

characters.name: Brock Butler
characters.description: Brock Butleris the father ofSean Butler.

characters.name: Bucky Dawes
characters.description: Bucky DawesInformationFull NameBucky DawesNickname(s)Mr. Bucky (used by Daphne)GenderMaleResides inNashvilleOccupationMaddie Conrad's manager, A&R Highway 65FriendsRayna James(client)†The Last HighwaysAffiliationsRayna Jaymes†InformationFirst EpisodePilotLast EpisodeBeyond the SunsetPortrayed ByDavid AlfordBucky Daweswas the manager of the lateRayna Jaymes.  Following her death, he became the manager of her daughterMaddie. Not much is known about him apart from him being Rayna's manager and one of her closest friends.  After she establishes Highway 65 he takes on the role of CFO of the company until her death following a car crash, which leaves him devastated. Zach Welles tells Deacon that in order for the label to move forward he needs to let go of Bucky but the latter decides to depart of his own accord.  He later becomesMaddie Conrad's manager.

She is kind of taken aback when he enquires aboutMaddiebeing home but she just dismisses it as being part of the perks of being famous.

characters.name: Cash Gray
characters.description: Cash GrayInformationFull NameCash GrayGenderFemaleOccupationSinger/SongwriterEye ColorBlueHair ColorBlondeFamilyFrankie Gray(father)RomancesMaddie Conrad(ex-friend)InformationFirst EpisodeWe've Got Nothing But Love to ProveLast EpisodeMaybe You'll Appreciate Me SomedayPortrayed ByJessy SchramCash Grayis the singer-songwriter daughter ofFrankie Gray. She was portrayed byJessy Schram. She helpsMaddiewith her songwriting, and sneaks the latter out to play some of her material at a bar. However,DaphnetellsDeaconwhere they are and he rescuesMaddiejust before she is molested by a man in the crowd. Her house is whereMaddiestays when she runs away from home. She also suggests getting emancipated and after it is granted she accompaniesMaddieto New York where she records her song. They meet producerVince Pierceand afterRaynareveals that he tried to assault herDeaconagain saved his daughter before the same could happen to her. He warned Cash to keep away from her andMaddiebroke off their friendship.

characters.description: Cash GrayInformationFull NameCash GrayGenderFemaleOccupationSinger/SongwriterEye ColorBlueHair ColorBlondeFamilyFrankie Gray(father)RomancesMaddie Conrad(ex-friend)InformationFirst EpisodeWe've Got Nothing But Love to ProveLast EpisodeMaybe You'll Appreciate Me SomedayPortrayed ByJessy SchramCash Grayis the singer-songwriter daughter ofFrankie Gray. She was portrayed byJessy Schram. She helpsMaddiewith her songwriting, and sneaks the latter out to play some of her material at a bar. However,DaphnetellsDeaconwhere they are and he rescuesMaddiejust before she is molested by a man in the crowd. Her house is whereMaddiestays when she runs away from home. She also suggests getting emancipated and after it is granted she accompaniesMaddieto New York where she records her song. They meet producerVince Pierceand afterRaynareveals that he tried to assault herDeaconagain saved his daughter before the same could happen to her. He warned Cash to keep away from her andMaddiebroke off their friendship.

characters.name: Charles Wentworth
characters.description: Charles WentworthInformationFull NameCharles WentworthGenderMaleOccupationBusinessmanRomancesJuliette Barnes(affair)Olivia Wentworth(ex-wife)InformationFirst EpisodeI Don't Wanna Talk About It NowLast EpisodeCrazyPortrayed ByCharlie Bewley Charles Wentworthis a wealthy businessman introduced inSeason Twowho had an affair withJuliette Barnes.  He was portrayed byCharlie Bewley. Julietteis hired by Charlie to perform at his and his wifeOlivia's fifth anniversary party. Charlie takes a liking to her. They soon have an extramarital affair, whichOliviadiscovers when she catches them having sex in a hotel room.  She later tellsJuliettethat she knows the truth and threatens to spill all to the media by kissing her to make it look that she's interested in threesomes.  She also intends to file for divorce.

characters.name: Clayton Carter
characters.description: Clayton CarterInformationFull NameClayton CarterGenderMaleOccupationSingerFriendsMaddie Conrad(ex-girlfriend)InformationFirst EpisodeLet's Put It Back Together AgainLast EpisodeA Change Would Do You GoodPortrayed ByJoseph David-Jones Clayton Carteris a street-wise musician who befriendsMaddie Conradand becomes her boyfriend. Introduced in theSeason FiveepisodeLet's Put It Back Together Again, he is a street musician whoMaddie Conradoverhears while going on a coffee run on her first day as an intern at the recording studio whereAvery Barkleyworks. She is impressed with his talent and they start chatting when she drops her coffee after leaving the coffee shop. He later visits the studio to return her lyrics' book that she left behind.

characters.name: Coleman Carlisle
characters.description: Coleman CarlisleInformationFull NameColeman Vincent CarlisleNickname(s)ColeGenderMaleResides inUnknownOccupationFormer Deputy MayorFamilyAudrey Carlisle(wife)RomancesAudrey Carlisle(wife)FriendsDeacon ClaybourneRayna JamesInformationFirst EpisodePilotLast EpisodeI Don't Wanna Talk About It NowPortrayed ByRobert Wisdom Coleman Carlislewas a politician and the Deputy Mayor of Nashville andDeacon Claybourne's first sobriety sponsor. He was portrayed byRobert Wisdom.  For 11 years, Coleman has served on Nashville’s Metro Council. Through hard work and dedication he's built a strong political base, but Coleman’s biggest backer has always beenLamar Wyatt, who employed Coleman’s mother as a housekeeper and who paid for Coleman’s college and law school. Coleman saw Lamar as a surrogate father and never dreamed that Lamar would cut him loose—until Lamar did recently for questioning the soundness of a new baseball stadium for Nashville. It’s a new rift that’ll test Coleman's resolve, character and family ties. It might also eventually cause him to turn on the mentor who made his success all possible.

characters.name: Colt Wheeler
characters.description: Colt WheelerInformationGenderMaleResides inNashvilleOccupationStudent/Army traineeHair ColorBlackFamilyLuke Wheeler(father)Unnamed motherSage Wheeler(sister)Robert Walker(maternal grandfather)RomancesMaddie Conrad(ex-girlfriend)InformationFirst EpisodeYour Wild Life's Gonna Get You DownLast EpisodeMaybe You'll Appreciate Me SomedayPortrayed ByKeean JohnsonColt Wheeleris the son ofLuke Wheelerand the brother ofSage Wheeler. He is first introduced toRaynaand her daughters by Luke. He has a huge social network following and seems interested in mixing songs (especially Hip-hop). While inMaddieandDaphne's company, he sees them performinga songand decides to add music to it. Unfortunately, it gets a lot of attention online and Luke forces him to take it down. Maddie soon takes a shine to him and they begin a relationship, much against their parents' wishes. At one stage, she suggests sleeping together but Colt isn't so sure. However, while on the road with Luke they end up sleeping together.

characters.name: Cy
characters.description: CyInformationGenderMaleOccupationLead singer of The Revel KingsInformationFirst EpisodeWhere He Leads MeLast EpisodeI'm Sorry for You My FriendPortrayed ByDaniel BuranCyis the lead singer of a group called the Revel Kings.  Appearing inSeason One, the band invitesDeacon Claybourneto be a backing musician on tour and he takes a shine toScarlett O'Connor. He attempts to sexually assault her beforeDeaconpunches him and tells him he's quitting the tour.

characters.name: Cynthia Davis
characters.description: Cynthia DavisInformationFull NameCynthia DavisGenderFemaleResides inAtlantaOccupationConservative punditEye ColorBlueHair ColorBlondeInformationFirst EpisodeThe Trouble with the TruthLast EpisodeMaybe You'll Appreciate Me SomedayPortrayed ByMandy June Turpin Cynthia Davisis a conservative pundit who is seen as an enemy of the gay community. Davis launches attacks onWill Lexingtonfor "promoting" homosexuality (even though Will has done nothing besides actually be gay), and forLuke Wheelerfor continuing to support him. After some considerable, Luke decides to make a stand against Cynthia's bigotry, and makes rounds on more liberal talk shows and media outlets. Cynthia, unable to rebut the positive publicity Luke has been receiving, doubles down on Will for recusing himself from the media.
